meta_title: Impact Doors in South Broward, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in South Broward, FL. ESW & CWI brands, in-house crews, $0 down financing. Call for a free estimate today!
h1: Impact Doors for South Broward Homes — Built to Outlast Every Storm Season
intro:
South Broward homeowners know better than most that hurricane season is not a distant threat — it's a yearly reality. Situated roughly five miles from the Atlantic coastline, neighborhoods throughout the 33023 zip code face sustained wind speeds that can top 170 mph during a major storm event. When a hurricane or severe tropical system bears down on Broward County, the difference between a home that weathers the storm and one that suffers catastrophic damage often comes down to one critical factor: the quality of your doors. Impact doors aren't a luxury upgrade here — they are a practical, code-required line of defense for one of South Florida's most storm-active communities.

With approximately 55% of South Broward's housing stock built before 1994, a significant share of homes in this area were constructed before Florida's modern hurricane protection standards took effect. Many of those older entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors simply were not engineered to handle the wind loads and flying debris that accompany a major storm. Homes near South Broward Park and throughout the surrounding residential corridors carry an average value of around $290,000 — a meaningful investment that deserves meaningful protection. Replacing aging, non-impact doors is one of the highest-ROI upgrades a homeowner in this area can make, protecting both the structure and the equity built up over years of ownership.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we specialize in helping South Broward families upgrade to high-performance impact doors that meet or exceed current Florida Building Code requirements. Whether you need a fortified entry door, a new sliding glass door for your back patio, or elegant French doors that still stand up to hurricane-force conditions, our team brings the expertise and product knowledge to get the job done right. As the Kings of Service, we've completed installations in over 500 homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties — and we're ready to protect yours.

section_why.h2: Why South Broward Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Door Installations
section_why.body:
Choosing the right contractor for impact door installation in South Broward is just as important as choosing the right product. Too many local homeowners have been burned by out-of-town companies that subcontract the work, cut corners on permitting, or disappear after the check clears. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing operates differently. Every installation we perform is completed entirely by our own in-house crews — no subcontractors, no third-party labor, no handoffs that compromise quality. Our technicians are trained specifically on the products we sell, meaning they understand every detail of how ESW and CWI impact doors are measured, fitted, sealed, and secured.

With over 60 years of combined experience across our installation and project management teams, we've seen virtually every type of home construction common to South Broward — from older CBS (concrete block structure) homes built in the 1960s and 70s to more recently constructed townhomes and single-family residences. That experience matters when it comes to proper framing, shimming, and sealing impact doors into openings that may have settled or shifted over decades. We know how to handle the unexpected, and we communicate with homeowners every step of the way so there are no surprises.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing holds an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau, is fully licensed and insured throughout Florida, and has built its reputation on the kind of service that earns referrals from neighbors, family members, and longtime clients. We pull all required permits through the Broward County Building Division, coordinate inspections on your behalf, and ensure every installation passes final review without issue. For homeowners in South Broward who want the job done right the first time — and who want a company they can call back if they ever have a question or concern — Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is the answer.

We also understand that South Broward is a community where value matters. Our customers are hardworking homeowners who want honest pricing, transparent timelines, and a finished product they can be proud of. We offer flexible financing options starting as low as $87 per month with $0 down, making it easier than ever to upgrade your home's doors without putting strain on your monthly budget.

section_history.h2: A Storm History That Demands Serious Door Protection in South Broward
section_history.body:
South Broward's storm history reads like a case study in why impact-rated products are not optional in this part of Florida. Since 1990, this community has experienced nine significant tropical storm or hurricane events, and three of those storms left lasting marks on the entire region. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 — the benchmark storm that rewrote Florida's building codes — devastated South Florida with Category 5 winds and exposed just how vulnerable pre-modern construction truly was. While Andrew's worst impacts were concentrated in Miami-Dade, Broward County communities felt the destruction as well, and the storm served as a sobering reminder of what unchecked wind pressure can do to doors, windows, and entire roof systems.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 swept across South Florida from west to east, delivering powerful gusts throughout Broward County and causing widespread damage to homes that lacked proper hurricane protection. Wilma was particularly notable for its rapid intensification and forward speed, which meant many homeowners had little time to prepare. Doors that weren't properly rated were blown in, allowing interior pressure to build and often contributing to roof failures. Hurricane Irma in 2017, while somewhat weakened by the time it reached Broward County, still brought sustained tropical storm and hurricane-force gusts that rattled unprotected doors, cracked frames, and in some cases allowed water intrusion that led to mold and structural damage.

These aren't distant historical events for South Broward residents — many homeowners in the 33023 zip code remember exactly where they were during each of these storms and what damage they personally witnessed or suffered. That lived experience is exactly why impact door upgrades remain one of the most in-demand home improvements in this community year after year.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in Broward County
section_code.body:
South Broward falls within Broward County's jurisdiction under the Florida Building Code, and while the 33023 area is not designated as part of the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — which applies to Miami-Dade and Broward's easternmost coastal communities — it is still subject to stringent wind-resistance requirements that mandate the use of hurricane-rated products. Homes in this area must meet wind speed design criteria of up to 170 mph, and all impact doors installed must carry appropriate product approvals and Miami-Dade or Florida Product Approval numbers to be compliant.

Every impact door installation in South Broward requires a permit pulled through the Broward County Building Division. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles this process entirely on behalf of our customers — from submitting the permit application and product documentation to scheduling all required inspections. We do not begin work without an approved permit in hand, and every installation is completed to pass final inspection without issue. Attempting to install impact doors without a permit not only puts homeowners at risk of fines and forced removal but can also create serious complications when filing insurance claims or selling the home in the future.

Impact doors installed in this wind zone must be tested and rated for both large missile impact (for openings below 30 feet) and pressure cycling, ensuring they can withstand both the physical force of windborne debris and the sustained positive and negative pressures a major storm produces. Products must comply with ASTM E1886 and ASTM E1996 testing standards at minimum. Our ESW and CWI product lines are fully tested, approved, and compliant with all applicable Florida Building Code requirements for Broward County, giving homeowners complete confidence that their installation meets the letter of the law.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Engineered for South Broward's Wind Zone
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing exclusively installs impact doors from two industry-leading manufacturers: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands have earned strong reputations throughout South Florida for their combination of structural performance, clean aesthetics, and long-term durability — exactly what South Broward homeowners need in a product that has to look great on a $290,000 home while standing up to 170 mph wind conditions.

ESW's impact door lineup includes reinforced entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French door configurations, all featuring heavy-duty aluminum frames with thermal break technology that helps reduce heat transfer — an important benefit in South Florida's intense climate. ESW doors use laminated impact-resistant glass that holds together even when struck by windborne debris, preventing the dangerous shattering that can occur with standard glazing. Their multi-point locking systems engage at multiple points along the door frame simultaneously, dramatically improving both security and wind resistance compared to traditional single-point locks. CWI offers a complementary range of impact door products with similar performance credentials, giving our customers a variety of style options and price points to match their home's architecture and budget.

Both ESW and CWI products carry the product approvals required for installation in Broward County, and both are manufactured with the specific needs of South Florida's coastal climate in mind — meaning corrosion-resistant hardware, UV-stable finishes, and weatherstripping designed to hold up against years of heat, humidity, and tropical rainfall. Whether you're replacing a single front entry door, upgrading a sliding glass door that leads to a back patio, or installing a set of French doors on a sunroom addition, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's product selection ensures we have the right solution for your home.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Doors Cost in South Broward — And How to Finance the Investment
section_cost.body:
The cost of impact door installation in South Broward varies depending on the number of openings, door type, size, and the specific products selected. For a single impact entry door, homeowners can typically expect to invest between $1,500 and $3,500 installed, while sliding glass door replacements commonly range from $2,000 to $5,000 depending on the width and glass configuration. Full-home door packages that include multiple entry points, sliders, and French doors can range from $8,000 to $20,000 or more for larger homes. These are not small expenditures, which is why transparent, honest pricing — and accessible financing — are core to how Premier Impact Windows & Roofing operates.

For South Broward homeowners working within a budget, our financing options are designed to make impact door upgrades accessible without delay. With plans starting at just $87 per month and $0 down options available for qualified buyers, many families are able to upgrade their home's protection immediately rather than waiting until they've saved enough cash. Given that impact doors can generate meaningful insurance savings and add measurable value to a home, the case for financing now rather than delaying is often very strong from a pure financial standpoint.

In terms of return on investment, impact doors are among the most cost-effective home improvements available to South Broward homeowners. Beyond the insurance discounts discussed in the next section, impact doors add real resale value in a market where buyers — especially those using FHA or VA financing — increasingly expect hurricane protection to already be in place. With the average South Broward home valued at approximately $290,000, even a modest increase in appraised value resulting from a full door upgrade can partially offset the cost of installation, making impact doors a financially sound decision in addition to a safety-conscious one.

section_insurance.h2: How Impact Doors Can Lower Your Homeowners Insurance Premiums in South Broward
section_insurance.body:
One of the most compelling and often underappreciated benefits of installing impact doors in South Broward is the potential for significant homeowners insurance savings. Florida's property insurance market is notoriously expensive, particularly in Broward County where hurricane risk elevates premiums for virtually every carrier. Impact doors — especially when combined with impact windows — can qualify homeowners for substantial wind mitigation credits that directly reduce the wind portion of their insurance premium, which is typically the largest component of a Florida homeowner's annual insurance cost.

The path to those savings begins with a wind mitigation inspection, performed by a licensed Florida inspector who evaluates your home's hurricane protection features and submits a standardized report to your insurance carrier. When that report confirms that all openings — including doors — are protected by impact-rated products, many carriers apply credits that can reduce wind premiums by 20% to 45% or more depending on the total scope of protection in place. For a South Broward homeowner paying $4,000 or more annually for homeowners insurance, those savings can add up to hundreds of dollars every year, potentially offsetting a significant portion of the original installation cost over time.

Common insurance carriers operating in Broward County — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and Heritage Insurance — all recognize wind mitigation improvements and apply credits in accordance with the Florida Office of Insurance Regulation's guidelines. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides customers with all the documentation needed to support a wind mitigation inspection, including product approval numbers, installation permits, and inspection records. We encourage every South Broward homeowner who completes an impact door installation with us to schedule a wind mitigation inspection promptly — the sooner you have that report in hand, the sooner your premium savings begin.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors if I'm not right on the coast in South Broward?
faqs.a: Absolutely. While South Broward's 33023 area sits about five miles from the Atlantic, it still falls in a 170 mph wind design zone under the Florida Building Code. Hurricane-force winds and windborne debris travel well inland, and homes in this area are required by code to have hurricane-rated doors and windows. Living away from the water does not reduce your legal obligation or your storm risk to a meaningful degree.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors typically cost, and what financing options does Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offer?
faqs.a: Pricing varies by door type and scope — a single entry door installation typically runs $1,500 to $3,500, while sliding glass doors or full-home packages can range higher. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners, making it possible to upgrade your protection immediately without a large upfront cash outlay.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to install impact doors in South Broward, and does Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handle that?
faqs.a: Yes, a permit is required for all impact door installations in Broward County, pulled through the Broward County Building Division. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf — from application and product documentation submission to coordinating all required inspections. We never begin work without an approved permit, ensuring your installation is fully code-compliant and properly documented for insurance and resale purposes.

faqs.q: How long does an impact door installation take in South Broward?
faqs.a: For most South Broward homes, a single impact door installation can be completed in a matter of hours, while larger projects involving multiple doors typically take one to two days. Lead times from contract signing to installation scheduling generally range from two to six weeks, depending on product availability and permit approval timelines. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing keeps customers informed throughout the process so there are no surprises.

faqs.q: Will installing impact doors actually lower my homeowners insurance in Broward County?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — especially when impact doors are part of a complete opening-protection strategy that includes impact windows as well. A wind mitigation inspection following your installation can document your upgrades for your insurance carrier, potentially qualifying you for wind premium credits of 20% to 45% or more. Many South Broward homeowners recover a significant portion of their installation cost through cumulative insurance savings over five to ten years.
